# New Project: Standalone SEO Service Tool

## Context

The user manages 10+ websites (starting with yurisbeautyparlor.com and hirecurve.com) and needs an internal SEO tool to manage SEO across all of them. Key constraints: free/minimal API budget, standalone Next.js app, Google Search Console already set up with one site (yurisbeautyparlor.com, 5 indexed pages, 3 not indexed, 0 clicks).

This is NOT a SaaS product for others; it is an internal tool for personal use.

## Tech Stack (matching HireCurve for familiarity)

- **Framework:** Next.js 15 (App Router) + TypeScript
- **Database:** PostgreSQL via Neon (serverless) + Prisma
- **UI:** Tailwind CSS v4, Lucide icons, Framer Motion
- **Auth:** Clerk (single-user, or simple env-based auth since internal)
- **AI:** Anthropic SDK (for content optimization suggestions)
- **APIs:** Google Search Console API (free), Google PageSpeed Insights API (free)
- **Deployment:** Vercel

## Core Features

### 1. Multi-Site Dashboard
- Add/remove sites with their domain, sitemap URL, and Google Search Console property
- Per-site health cards showing: indexed pages, crawl errors, avg position, clicks, impressions
- Global overview across all sites

### 2. Google Search Console Integration
- OAuth2 flow to connect GSC account
- Pull search analytics: queries, pages, clicks, impressions, CTR, position
- Track indexing status: indexed vs. not indexed pages
- Historical data trending (GSC provides 16 months of data)

### 3. Site Crawler (lightweight)
- Crawl sites on-demand to check: broken links (404s), missing meta tags, missing alt text, duplicate titles/descriptions, missing H1s, slow pages
- Store crawl results in DB for historical comparison
- Use fetch/cheerio (no headless browser needed for basic checks)

### 4. Keyword Rank Tracker
- Track target keywords per site using GSC data (free, no third-party rank API needed)
- Show position changes over time
- Group keywords by page

### 5. Content Optimization (AI-powered)
- Paste a page URL or content, get AI suggestions for: title tag optimization, meta description improvements, heading structure, keyword density, readability score
- Uses Anthropic API (minimal calls, cached results)

### 6. PageSpeed Monitor
- Periodic checks using Google PageSpeed Insights API (free, 25K requests/day)
- Track Core Web Vitals: LCP, FID/INP, CLS
- Desktop vs. mobile scores
- Historical trending

### 7. Competitor Tracking (basic)
- Add competitor URLs for comparison
- Compare page structure, meta tags, content length
- No paid APIs needed; uses crawler

## Database Schema (Prisma)

```prisma
model Site {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  domain        String   @unique
  name          String
  sitemapUrl    String?
  gscPropertyId String?  // Google Search Console property URI
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt

  crawls        Crawl[]
  pages         Page[]
  keywords      Keyword[]
  speedTests    SpeedTest[]
  searchData    SearchAnalytics[]
}

model Page {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  siteId      String
  url         String
  title       String?
  description String?
  h1          String?
  statusCode  Int?
  indexStatus  String?  // "indexed" | "not_indexed" | "error"
  lastCrawled DateTime?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt

  site        Site     @relation(fields: [siteId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  issues      PageIssue[]

  @@unique([siteId, url])
}

model PageIssue {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  pageId    String
  type      String   // "missing_meta" | "missing_alt" | "broken_link" | "duplicate_title" | "missing_h1" | "slow_page"
  severity  String   // "critical" | "warning" | "info"
  detail    String
  crawlId   String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  page      Page     @relation(fields: [pageId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  crawl     Crawl    @relation(fields: [crawlId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Crawl {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  siteId      String
  status      String   @default("pending") // "pending" | "running" | "completed" | "failed"
  pagesFound  Int      @default(0)
  issuesFound Int      @default(0)
  startedAt   DateTime @default(now())
  completedAt DateTime?

  site        Site        @relation(fields: [siteId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  issues      PageIssue[]
}

model Keyword {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  siteId    String
  keyword   String
  pageUrl   String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  site      Site     @relation(fields: [siteId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  rankings  KeywordRanking[]

  @@unique([siteId, keyword])
}

model KeywordRanking {
  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
  keywordId  String
  position   Float
  clicks     Int      @default(0)
  impressions Int     @default(0)
  ctr        Float    @default(0)
  date       DateTime

  keyword    Keyword  @relation(fields: [keywordId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([keywordId, date])
}

model SearchAnalytics {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  siteId      String
  date        DateTime
  query       String
  page        String
  clicks      Int      @default(0)
  impressions Int      @default(0)
  ctr         Float    @default(0)
  position    Float    @default(0)

  site        Site     @relation(fields: [siteId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([siteId, date, query, page])
}

model SpeedTest {
  id              String   @id @default(cuid())
  siteId          String
  url             String
  strategy        String   // "mobile" | "desktop"
  performanceScore Float
  lcp             Float?   // Largest Contentful Paint (ms)
  fid             Float?   // First Input Delay (ms)
  cls             Float?   // Cumulative Layout Shift
  inp             Float?   // Interaction to Next Paint (ms)
  ttfb            Float?   // Time to First Byte (ms)
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())

  site            Site     @relation(fields: [siteId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}
```

## Project Structure

```
seo-tool/
  src/
    app/
      layout.tsx
      page.tsx                    # Dashboard overview
      sites/
        page.tsx                  # Site list
        [siteId]/
          page.tsx                # Site detail
          crawl/page.tsx          # Crawl results
          keywords/page.tsx       # Keyword tracking
          speed/page.tsx          # PageSpeed results
          content/page.tsx        # Content optimizer
      api/
        sites/route.ts            # CRUD sites
        crawl/route.ts            # Trigger crawl
        crawl/[crawlId]/route.ts  # Crawl status
        gsc/
          auth/route.ts           # GSC OAuth callback
          sync/route.ts           # Pull GSC data
        speed/route.ts            # Run PageSpeed test
        content/optimize/route.ts # AI content suggestions
    lib/
      db.ts                       # Prisma client
      crawler.ts                  # Site crawler logic
      gsc.ts                      # Google Search Console API wrapper
      pagespeed.ts                # PageSpeed Insights API wrapper
      ai/content-optimizer.ts     # Anthropic-powered content suggestions
    components/
      site-card.tsx
      issue-badge.tsx
      keyword-chart.tsx
      speed-gauge.tsx
  prisma/
    schema.prisma
  package.json
  tsconfig.json
```

## Implementation Order

### Phase 1: Scaffold + Multi-Site Dashboard (Day 1)
1. `pnpm create next-app seo-tool --typescript --tailwind --app --src-dir`
2. Set up Prisma with Neon PostgreSQL
3. Create Site model and CRUD API routes
4. Build site list page and add-site form
5. Simple env-based auth (ADMIN_PASSWORD in .env, no Clerk needed for internal tool)

### Phase 2: Site Crawler (Day 2)
1. Implement crawler using fetch + cheerio
2. Parse HTML for: title, description, H1, images (alt text), links
3. Check for broken links (HEAD requests)
4. Store crawl results and page issues in DB
5. Build crawl results UI with issue severity badges

### Phase 3: Google Search Console Integration (Day 3)
1. Set up Google Cloud project with Search Console API enabled
2. Implement OAuth2 flow for GSC access
3. Build GSC data sync: search analytics, index status
4. Store in SearchAnalytics table
5. Build search performance charts (clicks, impressions, CTR, position over time)

### Phase 4: Keyword Tracking (Day 3-4)
1. Auto-extract keywords from GSC search analytics
2. Allow manual keyword additions
3. Track position changes over time from GSC data
4. Build keyword ranking charts and tables

### Phase 5: PageSpeed Monitor (Day 4)
1. Integrate PageSpeed Insights API v5
2. Run tests for mobile + desktop
3. Store Core Web Vitals history
4. Build speed dashboard with gauges and trends

### Phase 6: AI Content Optimizer (Day 5)
1. Build page content fetcher (fetch + cheerio)
2. Send to Anthropic API with SEO analysis prompt
3. Return: title suggestions, meta description, heading structure, keyword recommendations
4. Build content optimizer UI

### Phase 7: Polish + Deploy (Day 5)
1. Global dashboard with cross-site metrics
2. Responsive design
3. Deploy to Vercel
4. Connect custom domain (optional)

## API Costs (all free tier)

| API | Free Tier | Our Usage |
|-----|-----------|-----------|
| Google Search Console API | Unlimited (with quota) | ~100 requests/day |
| PageSpeed Insights API | 25,000 requests/day | ~50/day |
| Anthropic API | Pay per token | Minimal; cache results, ~$1-2/month |
| Neon PostgreSQL | Free tier: 0.5 GB | Sufficient for SEO data |
| Vercel | Free tier (hobby) | 1 project |

## Verification

1. Can add a site, trigger a crawl, and see issues listed
2. GSC OAuth works and pulls search analytics data
3. PageSpeed tests run and store Core Web Vitals
4. Keyword rankings show position changes over time
5. AI content optimizer returns actionable suggestions
6. All pages responsive on mobile
7. `pnpm tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm next lint` pass clean

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
  output   = "../src/generated/prisma"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
}

model Site {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  domain        String   @unique
  name          String
  sitemapUrl    String?
  gscPropertyId String?
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt

  crawls     Crawl[]
  pages      Page[]
  keywords   Keyword[]
  speedTests SpeedTest[]
  searchData SearchAnalytics[]
}

model Page {
  id          String    @id @default(cuid())
  siteId      String
  url         String
  title       String?
  description String?
  h1          String?
  statusCode  Int?
  indexStatus String?
  lastCrawled DateTime?
  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime  @updatedAt

  site   Site        @relation(fields: [siteId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  issues PageIssue[]

  @@unique([siteId, url])
}

model PageIssue {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  pageId    String
  type      String
  severity  String
  detail    String
  crawlId   String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  page  Page  @relation(fields: [pageId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  crawl Crawl @relation(fields: [crawlId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}

model Crawl {
  id          String    @id @default(cuid())
  siteId      String
  status      String    @default("pending")
  pagesFound  Int       @default(0)
  issuesFound Int       @default(0)
  startedAt   DateTime  @default(now())
  completedAt DateTime?

  site   Site        @relation(fields: [siteId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  issues PageIssue[]
}

model Keyword {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  siteId    String
  keyword   String
  pageUrl   String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  site     Site             @relation(fields: [siteId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  rankings KeywordRanking[]

  @@unique([siteId, keyword])
}

model KeywordRanking {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  keywordId   String
  position    Float
  clicks      Int      @default(0)
  impressions Int      @default(0)
  ctr         Float    @default(0)
  date        DateTime

  keyword Keyword @relation(fields: [keywordId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([keywordId, date])
}

model SearchAnalytics {
  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
  siteId      String
  date        DateTime
  query       String
  page        String
  clicks      Int      @default(0)
  impressions Int      @default(0)
  ctr         Float    @default(0)
  position    Float    @default(0)

  site Site @relation(fields: [siteId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([siteId, date, query, page])
}

model SpeedTest {
  id               String   @id @default(cuid())
  siteId           String
  url              String
  strategy         String
  performanceScore Float
  lcp              Float?
  fid              Float?
  cls              Float?
  inp              Float?
  ttfb             Float?
  createdAt        DateTime @default(now())

}

content.text:
Phase 1 is complete. Here's what was built:

**Project:** `seo-tool/` in your outputs folder

**What's in it:**

The project is a standalone Next.js 16 app (App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4) with Prisma 7 connected to Neon PostgreSQL.

**Files created (13 source files):**

**Database layer:**
- `prisma/schema.prisma` - Full schema with all 8 models (Site, Page, PageIssue, Crawl, Keyword, KeywordRanking, SearchAnalytics, SpeedTest)
- `prisma.config.ts` - Prisma 7 config pointing to `DATABASE_URL`
- `src/lib/db.ts` - Prisma client singleton using `@prisma/adapter-pg` (Prisma 7 pattern)

**API routes:**
- `src/app/api/sites/route.ts` - GET (list with stats) + POST (create with domain normalization, uniqueness check)
- `src/app/api/sites/[id]/route.ts` - GET (detail with relations) + PATCH (update) + DELETE (cascade)
- `src/app/api/auth/route.ts` - POST (login with cookie) + DELETE (logout)

**Auth:**
- `src/lib/auth.ts` - Header or cookie-based auth; disabled when `ADMIN_API_KEY` is empty (dev mode)

**UI:**
- `src/app/page.tsx` - Dashboard with global stats cards + site grid
- `src/app/sites/page.tsx` - Table view of all sites with actions menu
- `src/app/sites/[siteId]/page.tsx` - Site detail with stats, feature cards (crawler, GSC, PageSpeed), recent pages table, config panel
- `src/components/site-card.tsx` - Card with pages, issues, crawl status, speed score
- `src/components/add-site-dialog.tsx` - Modal form for adding sites
- `src/components/navbar.tsx` - Top nav with Dashboard/Sites links

**Verification:** `tsc --noEmit` and `eslint` both pass clean.

**To get it running:**
1. Create a Neon database and put the connection string in `.env` as `DATABASE_URL`
2. Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name init` to create tables
3. Run `npm run dev`

Ready for Phase 2 (Site Crawler) whenever you are.
